place: Laem Sor, Ko Samui District, Surat Thani, Thailand
title: Ayutthaya-Style Teakwood Beachfront Estate, Laem Sor
blurb: A barefoot-luxury estate sprawled across three acres of palm-dotted lawn running straight to a quiet, year-round swimmable beach — classic Ayutthaya-style teakwood pavilions and two converted rice barns, a beachside pool, a private tennis court and a waterfront sala.
detail:
Sprawled across three acres of rolling green dotted with swaying palms and manicured gardens that lead straight to a quiet, year-round swimmable beach, this is the private beachfront estate of your dreams. The charming Ayutthaya-style property is home to two luxuriously fitted teakwood pavilions housing seven bedrooms, plus two separate rice barns converted into individual bedrooms — gabled, tapered roofs and solid teak panelling showcasing the classic elegance of Thai architecture, antique furnishings gracefully balanced with contemporary pieces and bright pops of colour.

Every space, from the high-ceilinged living areas to the lush bedrooms, frames uninterrupted views of the pristine beach, turquoise water and nearby islands. Laze by the large beachside swimming pool or run the expansive lantern-lit lawns as you await an evening BBQ from your private chef and wait staff, or head to the waterfront sala after a game on the private tennis court. From the master Sairee Suite to the antique 'Big Barn' honeymoon suite, interconnected king rooms and children's bunk rooms, the home flexes from four to nine bedrooms for extended families and large groups.

Kayaks, table tennis, a yoga area and a scenic morning walk uphill to the nearby Wat Kao Chedi temple round out the stay, and the estate is a celebrated tropical wedding venue for up to 120 guests. Fully staffed with a villa manager, private chef and housekeeping, with a welcome drink, daily breakfast and WiFi throughout.
